note: Choose by layer first, then the specific tool. Layer comes from how the run behaves, not from brand preference.

layers.name: Visual automation
layers.blurb: Drag-and-drop flows that glue SaaS together. Fixed steps, one LLM node at most.
layers.whenChoose: Non-technical owner, deterministic steps, you are wiring apps together.

layers.name: Low-code agent platforms
layers.blurb: Hosted builders for RAG chatbots and single agents with a UI and connectors.
layers.whenChoose: You want an agent or assistant fast, with a console, without a codebase.

layers.name: Code-first agent frameworks
layers.blurb: Libraries where you write the agent: the model owns control flow, in your repo.
layers.whenChoose: Engineers, the model decides next steps, you need full control and tests.

layers.name: Durable orchestration engines
layers.blurb: Workflow engines that survive crashes, retries, and runs that last hours or days.
layers.whenChoose: Long-running, external actions, must outlive a restart, production reliability.

builders.name: Activepieces
builders.oneLiner: MIT, embeddable automation with an agent step.
builders.bestFor: Embeddable, open-source automation you can white-label.

builders.name: n8n
builders.oneLiner: Developer-friendly visual automation with code nodes.
builders.bestFor: Power users who want visual flows plus the escape hatch of code.

builders.name: Dify
builders.oneLiner: LLMOps platform: RAG, agents, and a prompt IDE.
builders.bestFor: RAG assistants and single agents with a full console.

builders.name: Flowise
builders.bestFor: Drag-and-drop chains and agents for fast prototypes.

builders.name: Lyzr
builders.oneLiner: Enterprise agent platform with a typed agent taxonomy.
builders.bestFor: Enterprise teams wanting governed agents off the shelf.

builders.name: CrewAI
builders.bestFor: Several specialist agents collaborating on a task.

builders.name: LangGraph
builders.oneLiner: Stateful graph control for agents, with checkpoints.
builders.bestFor: One capable agent with explicit, testable control flow and state.

builders.name: Orkes Conductor
builders.oneLiner: Durable workflow engine with human tasks and agentic steps.
builders.bestFor: Durable orchestration with built-in HITL and governance.

builders.name: Temporal
builders.oneLiner: Durable execution in code: retries and state for free.
builders.bestFor: Engineers who want durable, code-first long-running workflows.

questions.label: Who builds and owns it?
questions.help: The team that maintains the thing day to day.

questions.label: Who owns the control flow?
questions.help: The single biggest fork: a fixed pipeline, or the model deciding what to do next.

questions.label: Does it take real-world actions?
questions.help: Calling external APIs, moving data, sending things.

options.label: Yes, it acts on outside systems

questions.label: How long is a run, and must it survive a crash?
questions.help: Durability is the reason to reach for a workflow engine.

options.label: Minutes to days, or must outlive a restart

questions.label: Reliability bar?
questions.help: Money, irreversible actions, and external users raise it.

modelTiers.name: Tiered with fallback
modelTiers.desc: Haiku for classification and routing, Sonnet for reasoning and tool calls, Opus as the fallback for hard cases. Claude leads on tool-heavy steps. Always define a fallback chain.

modelTiers.name: Lean default
modelTiers.desc: Sonnet as the workhorse, Haiku for the cheap deterministic steps. Add a one-line fallback so a provider outage does not take you down.

memoryTypes.name: No memory
memoryTypes.desc: Stateless per run. Cheapest and most predictable. Start here.

memoryTypes.name: Working memory
memoryTypes.desc: Carries personalization and state within a session or task.

memoryTypes.name: Semantic recall (RAG)
memoryTypes.desc: Retrieve facts from a corpus at query time. Use for factual recall, not as default memory.

memoryTypes.name: Observational memory
memoryTypes.desc: Accumulates from tool-heavy, long-running sessions. Append, never wholesale-replace.

evalApproaches.name: Task-completion eval
evalApproaches.desc: Did it finish the job correctly against a golden dataset, scored by code not a raw LLM number.

evalApproaches.name: Tool-calling eval
evalApproaches.desc: Did it pick the right tool with the right arguments. Critical when the agent acts on systems.

evalApproaches.name: Faithfulness eval
evalApproaches.desc: Is the answer grounded in the retrieved context, no hallucination. For reason-and-answer agents.

evalApproaches.name: Multi-turn eval
evalApproaches.desc: Does it hold context and goal across a conversation.
